title: Cacalia seemanniana

It belongs to the tribe Eupatorieae, a group of plants that are primarily found in the Americas. The genus Cacalia is a small group of plants that are native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. intro: Cacalia seemanniana, also known as Cacalia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia seemanniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
